Reading

Philosophy

  • Tao Te Ching
  • Art of War

Life

  • The Places That Scare You: A Guide to Fearlessness in Difficult Times, Pema Chödrön
  • Comfortable with Uncertainty: 108 Teachings on Cultivating Fearlessness and Compassion, Pema Chödrön
  • The Courage to Be Disliked: The Japanese Phenomenon That Shows You How to Change Your Life and Achieve Real Happiness, Ichiro Kishimi and Fumitake Koga
  • The Heart of the Buddha’s Teaching: Transforming Suffering into Peace, Joy, and Liberation, Thich Nhat Hanh
  • Peace Is Every Step: The Path of Mindfulness in Everyday Life, Thich Nhat Hanh
  • The Art of Happiness, His Holiness the Dalai Lama
  • Crucial Conversations Tools for Talking When Stakes Are High, Kerry Patterson, Joseph Grenny, Ron McMillan, Al Switzler
  • Never Split the Difference: Negotiating As If Your Life Depended On It, Chris Voss
  • Thinking, Fast and Slow, Daniel Kahneman
  • The Life-Changing Magic of Tidying Up: The Japanese Art of Decluttering and Organizing, Marie Kondo
  • The Effective Executive: The Definitive Guide to Getting the Right Things Done, Peter F. Drucker
  • Deep Work: Rules for Focused Success in a Distracted World, Cal Newport
  • Influence: The Psychology of Persuasion, Robert B. Cialdini

Death

  • On Death and Dying: What the Dying Have to Teach Doctors, Nurses, Clergy and Their Own Families, Elisabeth Kubler-Ross
  • How We Die: Reflections on Life’s Final Chapter, Sherwin B. Nuland
  • The Tibetan Book of Living and Dying, Sogyal Rinpoche

History

  • Sapiens: A Brief History of Humankind, Yuval Noah Harari

Environment

  • Voluntary Simplicity: Toward a Way of Life That Is Outwardly Simple, Inwardly Rich, Duane Elgin
  • Small Is Beautiful: Economics as if People Mattered, E. F. Schumacher
  • Silent Spring, Rachel Carson
  • Natural Capitalism, Amory B. Lovins, L. Hunter Lovins, Paul Hawken
  • Cradle to Cradle: Remaking the Way We Make Things, William McDonough, Michael Braungart

Design

  • The Design of Everyday Things, Donald A. Norman
  • Flow: The Psychology of Optimal Experience, Mihaly Csikszentmihalyi
  • Visual Intelligence: Sharpen Your Perception, Change Your Life, Amy E. Herman

Health

  • Whole: Rethinking the Science of Nutrition, T. Colin Campbell PhD/PhD
  • Rest: Why You Get More Done When You Work Less, Alex Soojung-Kim Pang
  • Why We Sleep: Unlocking the Power of Sleep and Dreams, Matthew Walker
  • How Not to Die: Discover the Foods Scientifically Proven to Prevent and Reverse Disease, Michael Greger MD and Gene Stone

Biography

  • Autobiography of Malcolm X, Alex Haley and Attallah Shabazz Malcolm X
  • Is That It?, Bob Geldof
  • Autobiography of a Tibetan Monk,Palden Gyatso
  • My Land and My People: The Original Autobiography of His Holiness the Dalai Lama of Tibet, His Holiness the Dalai Lama
  • An Autobiography or The Story of My Experiments with Truth, M. K. Gandhi
  • Long Walk to Freedom: The Autobiography of Nelson Mandela, Nelson Mandela
  • A Heartbreaking Work Of Staggering Genius: A Memoir Based on a True Story, Dave Eggers

Fiction

  • Ishmael:A Novel, Daniel Quinn
  • Song of Solomon, Toni Morrison
  • The Alchemist, Paulo Coelho
  • Siddartha, Hermann Hesse
  • God of Small Things, Arundhati Roy
  • Things Fall Apart, Chinua Achebe
  • I Know Why the Caged Bird Sings, Maya Angelou
  • Color Purple, Alice Walker
  • To Kill a Mockingbird, Harper Lee
  • As I Lay Dying, William Faulkner
  • The Sound and the Fury, William Faulkner
  • What Is the What, Dave Eggers
  • The Circle, Dave Eggers
